title: Review-first agent workflow pattern
summary: A short public article describes how review queues help teams keep AI-drafted work accountable before it reaches customers or public channels.
why_it_matters: The pattern maps directly to safer publishing, research, and outreach systems because the model drafts while a human approves the final action.
next_action: Adapt as a public checklist for review-gated AI workflows.

title: Source trail checklist for weekly briefs
summary: A lightweight checklist shows how to keep titles, URLs, dates, visibility labels, and decisions attached to each research item.
why_it_matters: Clear source trails make weekly briefs easier to audit and safer to reuse in public summaries.
next_action: Use the schema as a fake-data example for public documentation.

title: Unverified model release discussion
summary: A public discussion mentions a possible model release, but the claim is not confirmed by an official source.
why_it_matters: Research briefs should separate useful signals from unverified claims so readers do not mistake rumors for facts.
next_action: Hold until an official release note or documentation page confirms the claim.
review_note: Needs source verification before public reuse.

title: Duplicate background note
summary: Older background note covering the same review queue concept as a newer source.
why_it_matters: Duplicates can be archived so the brief stays short and decision-ready.
